intro: Apium graveolens liniment, also known as celery seed oil, is a topical remedy derived from the seeds of the Apium graveolens plant. Traditionally used in traditional Chinese medicine, it is believed to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making it effective in relieving muscle and joint pain. However, its effectiveness and safety for human use have not been extensively scientifically proven, and more research is needed to fully understand its benefits and potential side effects.
side_effects: Apium graveolens liniment can have side effects such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and potential interactions with medications. These side effects may vary in severity and are often dependent on individual factors, such as skin sensitivity or underlying health conditions. It is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using apium graveolens liniment to minimize risks.

recipe: To make apium graveolens liniment for medicinal use, start by infusing fresh parsley seeds (Apium graveolens) in a carrier oil such as olive or coconut oil for several weeks to allow the herbal properties to extract. Strain the mixture and discard the solids. Store the infused oil in a dark glass container and use it as a topical liniment to relieve muscle and joint pain.
